channel.title: Kids on Have an average day :|
channel.description: Recent content in Kids on Have an average day :|

item.title: Last day school for the 2014-2015 year!
item.description: It’s the last day of school and Avriel won a bike! No one seems to know why but everyone seems to know I’m her dad and needed to tell me… I’m okay with that. The kids have had a lot of ups and downs over this year. In February they were wild, unruly, and were looking at being held back a grade. We worked really hard and got them to where they needed to be.

item.title: Goodbye K hello 1st!
item.description: [Xan is moving on from kindergarten into first grade! He made a great performance but sadly most of the pictures were out of focus. It was also really nice that Mommy was to make it too. I know it made Xan very happy to see her there.

item.title: Animal Kingdom
item.description: Through generosity of friends the kids and I were able to go to Animal Kingdom.The children had no idea what was going to happen and I managed to keep it a secret the entire time, which was very hard to do. They nearly figured it out a few times and I had to distract them. I told them we were going to stay with a friend of mine and hang out with them for Spring break.

item.title: So I adopted some rats.
item.description: So I went and did something really impulsive. My kids have begged for a pet. Paying a pet fee and keeping up with all the other bills that go with dogs & cats are just way more than I can afford. Plus I’m not around enough to make sure they get trained correctly. So that leaves me smaller things. I could have gotten a turtle, hamsters, etc. I opted to get them rats.

item.title: Poking fun at Linux Gaming
item.description: So I see this post on Reddit and thought it was pretty funny. I didn’t laugh till I saw the top comment though. AbaddonSF: This must be the Ubuntu version, Slack or gentoo, would be a log, and some copper ore. Hypersapien: A log? Pfft. You want all the work done for you? Try an acorn. You’ll need iron ore and coal as well to make the steel tools to cut the tree down when it grows and shape the wood.

item.title: Thanksgiving!
item.description: I’m going to try to update this throughout today. I doubt that’s going to happen though. Morning: Up late last night getting everything ready. I’m looking forward to seeing the kids reactions to the food and movies. My brother should make an appearance too. The kids really love Uncle Adam, even though they don’t see him nearly enough. Then again they don’t see a lot of family nearly enough. I do miss the days of being around a large family with 20+ people around the table for Sunday Dinner.

item.title: Yea, who needs a couch?
item.description: My kids are having a great time. Watching Puss in Boots. A $30 tent makes for an awesome blanket fort.

item.title: And whoever wrote the Bond-style songs for Special Agent Oso needs to be taken out by a grease dumpster and murdered in three special steps.
item.description: Oh how true this is. And whoever wrote the Bond-style songs for Special Agent Oso needs to be taken out by a grease dumpster and murdered in three special steps. via Children’s Music Will Destroy Your Good Taste Forever; Or, How I Came To Like Kidz Bop.

item.title: SassyGirl LOVES her Easter Candy
item.description: Daddy: Eat your dinner or I’m going to call the Easter Bunny and tell him that Easter is canceled Mommy: And he can eat all of your candy! Artsy Girl: NOOOO TheBoy: NOOOO SassyGirl: I’ll KILL THAT BUNNY!

item.title: Kids...
item.description: Kids, their enjoyment is directly proportional to your frustration.

item.title: The Desitin Incident.
item.description: I get a call at work this morning…. Iruma: Your son just ate and an entire tube of desitin. Me: ….. Iruma: hehe Me: Did you call poison control? Iruma: Yes (still laughing) Me: AND!? Iruma: He’ll be fine and might have an upset stomach. You need to fix the baby gate. Me: AGAIN!?
